title: Main irrigation pump degradation
summary: The greenhouse shows unstable irrigation behavior that may be linked to degradation of the main irrigation pump.
suspected_issue: Possible degradation of the main irrigation pump affecting irrigation stability.
symptoms: Main line pressure is unstable during irrigation windows.
Flow is lower than expected for the active irrigation program.
Some irrigation cycles appear to finish with uneven sector coverage.
The issue affects a production-phase tomato greenhouse.

observed_signals.interpretation: Pressure is below the expected operating range for stable irrigation.
Flow is below the expected range and may indicate pumping or distribution weakness.
Tank level is adequate, so low supply volume is not the main suspected cause.

operational_context.note: The greenhouse is currently operating under normal daytime irrigation schedule.
operational_context.impact: The issue is occurring during routine production conditions, not during an exceptional test.

operational_context.note: A backup pump is installed and available.
operational_context.impact: There is a fallback path that may reduce operational risk if the main pump is confirmed to be degraded.

agronomic_context.note: The crop is in production phase.
agronomic_context.impact: Irrigation instability may affect yield consistency and fruit quality.

business_context.note: The greenhouse is in regular production mode with no declared harvest emergency.
business_context.impact: The case is important but does not yet indicate an exceptional commercial emergency.

notes: This initial MVP case is designed to represent a realistic irrigation-related decision situation with sufficient visibility and a clear fallback option.
